mission.title: Install and Configure Kubean on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kubean is a production-ready cluster lifecycle management toolchain based on Kubespray, designed to simplify the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters. Installing Kubean allows you to leverage its powerful lifecycle management features for your Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Create Kubean Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Kubean to operate in.

steps.title: Apply Kubean Manifests
steps.description: Download and apply the Kubean manifests from the GitHub repository.

steps.title: Verify Kubean Installation
steps.description: Check that Kubean pods are running successfully.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kubean will show all pods running in the kubean namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods -n kubean`. Additionally, the resource limits and RBAC configurations will be applied as specified.

uninstall.title: Remove Kubean Resources
uninstall.description: Delete the Kubean resources and namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubean
upgrade.description: To upgrade Kubean, apply the new version manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pods Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: Check the pod logs for errors using: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubean`.

troubleshooting.title: Kubean Namespace Issues
troubleshooting.description: Ensure the kubean namespace exists: `kubectl get namespaces`.

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas
troubleshooting.description: Verify if there are resource quotas in place that might be preventing pod creation: `kubectl get resourcequotas -n kubean`.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
